edge_cases.title: Subagent session — no user messages — task window undefined
edge_cases.expected: Hook 2 must pass through when the transcript has no user messages (subagent session context). It must never block all subagent mutations or crash.
edge_cases.test_stub:
def test_hook2_subagent_transcript_no_user_messages():
    transcript = [
        {"type": "tool_call", "tool_name": "Read", "tool_input": {"file_path": "/x.py"}},
        {"type": "tool_result", "content": "file contents"},
    ]
    result = run_hook2_with_transcript(transcript, tool_name="Edit")
    assert result["action"] == "allow"  # currently: unspecified / may crash or block

edge_cases.title: Empty repo (zero commits) — git rev-parse HEAD returns exit 128
edge_cases.expected: Hook 3 must pass through when git rev-parse HEAD fails (exit code 128, no commits in repo). Must not block with garbled HEAD=(empty string) message.
edge_cases.test_stub:
def test_hook3_git_no_commits(tmp_git_empty_repo, final_audit_with_verdict_sha):
    result = run_hook3(
        command="git commit -m 'init'",
        cwd=tmp_git_empty_repo,
        audit_files=[final_audit_with_verdict_sha],
    )
    assert result["action"] == "allow"  # currently: blocks with garbled empty-string HEAD

edge_cases.title: Short SHA in verdict_sha vs full 40-char HEAD — permanent false block
edge_cases.expected: Hook 3 must normalize SHAs before comparison. A verdict_sha of '1424797' (7-char short) must compare equal to HEAD '1424797ed402f266d67fd54cdf0f1781dc370181' (40-char). Must not false-block when the user is at the same commit the verdict covered.
edge_cases.test_stub:
def test_hook3_short_verdict_sha_same_commit(repo_at_sha, final_audit_short_sha):
    result = run_hook3(
        command="git commit --amend --no-edit",
        verdict_sha_in_file="1424797",
        current_head_full="1424797ed402f266d67fd54cdf0f1781dc370181",
    )
    assert result["action"] == "allow"  # currently: blocks (short != full comparison fails)

edge_cases.title: Truncated JSONL line in transcript (partial-write race) — per-line JSONDecodeError crashes scan
edge_cases.expected: scan_transcript_lines must catch JSONDecodeError per line, skip the malformed line, and return all fully-parsed events. Must not propagate the exception to the fail-open wrapper.
edge_cases.test_stub:
def test_transcript_scan_truncated_last_line():
    raw = (
        '{"type": "tool_call", "tool_name": "Skill", "tool_input": {"skill": "tlmforge:feature-development"}}\n'
        '{"type": "tool_call", "tool_name": "Edit", "tool_i'  # truncated
    )
    events = list(scan_transcript_lines(raw))
    skill_found = any(e.get("tool_name") == "Skill" for e in events)
    assert skill_found is True
    assert len(events) == 1  # currently: raises JSONDecodeError

edge_cases.title: Override false positives — 'minimal' and 'trivial' in normal technical sentences
edge_cases.expected: detect_override() must return False for 'the trivial solution is O(n^2)' and 'use minimal logging'. These are common technical phrases that do not represent intent to bypass discipline.

edge_cases.title: Hook 3 glob uses cwd relative path — fails when claude is run from a subdirectory
edge_cases.expected: Hook 3 must anchor the specs/ glob to the git repo root (via git rev-parse --show-toplevel), not to cwd. Must find and enforce Stage 5 verdicts even when cwd is a project subdirectory.
edge_cases.test_stub:
def test_hook3_finds_audit_from_subdirectory(tmp_repo_with_audit):
    src_dir = tmp_repo_with_audit / "src"
    src_dir.mkdir()
    result = run_hook3(
        command="git push origin main",
        cwd=src_dir,
        head_sha="newsha",
        verdict_sha_in_audit="oldsha",
    )
    assert result["action"] == "block"  # currently: passes through (glob finds nothing from src/)

edge_cases.title: TLMFORGE_HOOKS bypass — only '0' accepted; 'false', 'off', '' do not bypass
edge_cases.expected: is_hooks_enabled() must return False for all conventional 'disabled' values: '0', 'false', 'False', 'FALSE', 'no', 'off', and empty string. Exact equality to '0' is insufficient.

edge_cases.title: verdict_sha unreachable after git rebase — git rev-list crashes Hook 3 into fail-open
edge_cases.expected: When verdict_sha is not in the current git history (e.g., after a rebase rewrote SHAs), Hook 3 must still BLOCK (not crash into fail-open). The commit count in the message may show '?' but the block must fire.
edge_cases.test_stub:
def test_hook3_verdict_sha_unreachable_after_rebase(tmp_repo):
    result = run_hook3(
        command="git commit -m 'post-rebase'",
        verdict_sha_in_audit="deadbeef00000000000000000000000000000000",
        current_head="newsha",
    )
    assert result["action"] == "block"  # currently: git rev-list crashes -> fail-open -> allows
